news.headline: EZ-ACCESS sponsors "Make America Healthy Again” round table in Kentucky to address healthcare & accessibility, showing commitment to improving home, work, and play access.
news.summary: In Hopkinsville, Kentucky, a recent Round Table event showcased EZ-ACCESS' dedication to enhancing accessibility in homes, workplaces, and recreational spaces. The sponsorship of the event underscores the company's commitment to improving access for all individuals.

news.headline: Germany's loyalty market has seen 11.7% CAGR during 2019-2023, expected to continue growing at 8%. New programs like PAYBACK Enhancements and Lidl Plus are in focus.
news.summary: A report titled "Germany Loyalty Programs Market Intelligence and Future Growth Dynamics Databook" has been included in ResearchAndMarkets.com's offerings. The German loyalty market experienced a 11.7% CAGR from 2019 to 2023. Forecasts suggest that the market will continue expanding, with an expected CAGR of 8 in the upcoming years. This report provides insights on loyalty program trends across various sectors, operational KPIs, retail product dynamics, and consumer demographics.

news.headline: Abbott Laboratories boasts consistent market-beating returns as the top-returning healthcare stock since 1937, reporting 4.9% growth in Q3.
news.summary: A study by Arizona State University's finance professor, Hendrik Bessembinder, revealed that Abbott Laboratories (NYSE: ABT) ranks 11th in top-returning stock since 1937 and is #1 in the healthcare sector. The key takeaway is Abbott's consistent market-beating returns due to slow, steady growth over the long term. Factors driving its value include company growth, cash flow, dividends, and share repurchases. Abbott's stock also helps reduce portfolio risk with a low beta of 0.7x the S&P 500. In Q3, Abbott reported 4.9% growth despite lower COVID-related sales, with medical devices growing by 11.7% reported and 13.3% organic. This growth signifies Abbott's high-quality stock and value for investors.

news.headline: Hacker who faked SEC Bitcoin ETF approval arrested by FBI. Eric Council Jr. charged with ID theft and fraud.
news.summary: Eric Council Jr. has been accused of plotting to carry out aggravated identity theft and access device fraud, according to legal charges brought against him.

news.headline: Finance website hacked; users urged to wait for fix. Malware spread via Python Package Index, texts, macOS, & emails in 2024.
news.summary: In the year 2024, a significant number of systems were infiltrated by malware distributed through various channels, such as the Python Package Index, text messages, deceitful macOS applications, and automated email threads.

news.headline: Spot Bitcoin ETFs see $20B inflows in 4th day, open dams flood with money since launching in January.
news.summary: Over the past four days, Spot Bitcoin ETFs have seen continuous inflows, leading to a total of more than $20 billion in cumulative flows since their introduction in January.

news.headline: Former FTX engineering director, Nishad Singh, requests reduced sentence for playing 'limited' part in crimes. Pled guilty to fraud, conspiracy charges in Feb. 2023. Next court appearance set for Oct. 30.
news.summary: In February 2023, a former engineering director at FTX admitted guilt to charges of fraud and conspiracy. They are set to appear in court for a sentencing hearing on October 30.

news.headline: Gnosis Pay offers self-custodial crypto debit card, uniting banking with Web3 tech for a new era in payments.

This innovation marks a significant leap in the financial sector, offering users the convenience of legacy payment infrastructure alongside the cutting-edge technology of Web3.

news.headline: DevRev upgrades SaaS platform with conversational interface, promises improved enterprise workflow.
news.summary: DevRev, a company based in Palo Alto, California, has unveiled product upgrades aimed at revolutionizing enterprise SaaS with advanced conversational AI capabilities. These enhancements are anticipated to streamline work processes for businesses. Despite the enthusiasm surrounding AI in enterprise settings, companies have encountered barriers such as disjointed systems, AI-human workflow compatibility issues, and data disorganization. The new updates from DevRev are set to tackle these challenges, with a focus on enhancing user experience and efficiency. This development signifies a pivotal moment for the enterprise sector, as organizations seek to leverage AI technologies for improved operations.

news.headline: San Manuel Band of Mission Indians and Yaamava' Resort & Casino win top awards at 2024 Global Gaming Awards - Responsible Business and Property of the Year.
news.summary: A prestigious award was given to the San Manuel Band of Mission Indians and Yaamava' Resort & Casino at San Manuel in Highland, California on October 17, 2024. They were named Responsible Business of the Year and Property of the Year - North America, marking their outstanding achievement in gaming and hospitality. This move aims to provide operators with the ability to utilize the newest technology. The announcement was made on October 17, 2024.

news.headline: SGS North America expands food and nutraceutical testing capacity to serve North American markets with new state-of-the-art facilities. (Character count: 118)
news.summary: In Fairfield, New Jersey, on October 17, 2024, SGS North America (SGS) revealed an enhancement in its food safety and quality assurance testing capabilities for the North American food and nutraceutical sectors. The expansion includes relocating to a more spacious, cutting-edge facility, demonstrating the company's commitment to serving the region.

news.headline: DaVita names Jessica Hergenreter as new Chief People Officer to lead People Services team.
news.summary: On October 17, 2024, in Denver, DaVita, a prominent provider of kidney care services, revealed Jessica (Jess) Hergenreter as their newly appointed chief people officer (CPO). Hergenreter will now supervise DaVita's People Services team, spearheading efforts to enhance engagement and morale within the company.

news.headline: Maximus secures spot on $300M IRS consulting services Blanket Purchase Agreement for Non-IT consulting services.
news.summary: Tysons, Virginia - Maximus has secured a position on a Blanket Purchase Agreement with the IRS for Non-IT Consulting Services. This new agreement is significant for Maximus as it opens up opportunities to provide consulting services to the IRS. The agreement allows Maximus to offer a wide range of consulting services to support the IRS in various non-IT projects. This achievement reflects Maximus's expertise and capabilities in providing consulting services, and it is expected to contribute positively to the company's growth and revenue.

news.headline: OPEXUS to host 19th Annual Summit in Washington on Oct 29, coinciding with FOIAXpress' 25th anniversary, expecting 200+ attendees. "Better Together" theme focuses on building trust and accountability in public institutions.
news.summary: An industry leader in government case management software, OPEXUS, has revealed details about its upcoming 19th Annual Summit in Washington, D.C., scheduled for October 29. The summit holds special significance this year as it aligns with the 25th anniversary of the company's flagship product, FOIAXpress, which dominates the federal market and serves over 150 government agencies. The event is expected to host more than 200 government professionals dedicated to fostering trust and ensuring accountability in public institutions. Titled "Better Together," this year's summit aims to emphasize the importance of collaboration and unity for effective governance.

news.headline: Untether AI leading energy-efficient AI acceleration in agriculture, tackling labor shortages, inefficiency. Driving productivity and sustainability.
news.summary: In Toronto, Untether AI®, a prominent company specializing in energy-centric AI acceleration technology, is paving the way for innovation in agriculture. By offering edge-optimized solutions, they are tackling pressing issues such as labor shortages and inefficient resource utilization. Through the implementation of AI in farming practices, Untether AI is revolutionizing the sector by enhancing productivity and sustainability. Their cutting-edge solutions are set to transform agriculture, ensuring a promising future for the industry.

news.headline: Alcoa beats Q3 earnings, reports $2.904B sales, 57 cents EPS (exceeding consensus of 28 cents), forecasts 9.8-10M metric tons production in 2024.

However, their adjusted EPS of 57 cents surpassed the consensus of 28 cents. The company is maintaining its 2024 Alumina segment production forecast at 9.8 to 10 million metric tons and has increased its shipment projection to 12.9 to 13.1 million metric tons.

news.headline: ERI enhances ITAD services in Indiana with AI, robotics, and battery recycling; operates eight e-waste facilities nationwide.

This facility is equipped with advanced technologies like AI-driven robotics, hyper-efficient shredding systems, and smart material tracking and reporting capabilities.

Brokerage firms recommend e.l.f. Beauty (NYSE: ELF) with an average rating of 1.38 out of 5. 81.3% of 16 recommendations advise a Strong Buy.
news.summary: The news article discusses the influence of Wall Street analysts' recommendations on stock decisions, focusing on e.l.f. Beauty (NYSE: ELF). The average brokerage recommendation for ELF is 1.38 on a scale from 1 to 5, with 13 out of 16 recommendations being Strong Buy (81.3%). Despite this, studies suggest that relying solely on brokerage recommendations may not lead to the best investment decisions due to potential conflicts of interest. Investors are encouraged to consider other factors when making investment choices. The full story is available on Benzinga.com.

The new service line is focused on accelerating the growth of these startups in the digital health sector.

news.headline: Global CSTD market expected to reach USD 3.6 billion by 2034 with a CAGR of 10.0%. Public awareness promotes growth.
news.summary: The global closed system transfer devices (CSTD) market was valued at US$ 1.2 billion in 2023 and is projected to reach US$ 3.6 billion by 2034, growing at a CAGR of 10.0%. The rise in usage of CSTDs is attributed to public awareness campaigns on handling hazardous medications safely. CSTDs aid in maintaining pharmaceutical purity and preventing contamination during medication transfer and administration. Patient safety is a key priority in the medical field, and CSTDs contribute to improving safety outcomes during drug delivery procedures. The increase in healthcare spending, particularly in developed countries, is driving the adoption of technologies like CSTDs for patient safety and care. North America held a dominant position in the global CSTD market in 2023. Key players in the market include ICU Medical, Baxter, B. Braun, BD, Equashield, Yukon Medical, Caragen Ltd., and JMS Co. Ltd. Key findings from the market report suggest that the system segment is expected to drive the market, the nursing segment will be a major market for CSTDs, and increasing distributor demand will boost the market further.

news.headline: Palomar Health partners with IKS Health for healthcare technology & services transformation; multi-year deal into next decade.
news.summary: A multi-year partnership has been formed between Palomar Health, a prominent healthcare system in North San Diego County, CA, and IKS Health, a provider of technology-based healthcare solutions. This collaboration is set to extend into the next decade and aims to utilize cutting-edge technology facilitated by Gen. This platform supports various health systems and physician enterprises across the U.S., Canada, and Australia. The partnership signifies a significant stride in enhancing healthcare services with innovative solutions.

news.headline: Rubis sells 55% stake in Rubis Terminal, now Tepsa.
FBI arrests Eric Council Jr. in SEC X account hack linked to Bitcoin ETF spike, causing BTC value to rise by $1,000.
news.summary: A 25-year-old Alabama man named Eric Council Jr. has been arrested by the FBI for his involvement in the January 2024 hack of the Security and Exchange Commission’s X account. This hack involved falsely announcing that Bitcoin ETFs were authorized for trading, causing Bitcoin’s value to increase by $1,000. Following the incident, the SEC did approve the ETFs the next day. The U.S. Attorney’s Office for the District of Columbia has reported that Council is facing charges of conspiracy to commit aggravated identity theft and access device fraud. On January 9, Council and his co-conspirators allegedly gained unauthorized access to the @SECgov X account and made fraudulent posts.

news.headline: California housing demand dips, with existing home sales at 253,010 in September (3.4% drop from August). Median price: $868,150.
news.summary: In September, there were 253,010 existing single-family home sales on a seasonally adjusted annualized rate. This marked a 3.4 percent decrease from August's 262,050 sales but a 5.1 percent increase from September 2023's 240,840 sales. The median home price in September was reported at $868,150 statewide, showing a 2.3 percent decline from August.

news.headline: Josh Johnstone appointed VP of Marketing at Valor PayTech, with 25+ years experience in the field. #marketing #payments
news.summary: Valor PayTech, based in Jericho, New York, has appointed Josh Johnstone as its new Vice President of Marketing. With over 25 years of marketing experience, Josh will spearhead the company's marketing efforts to bolster its position as a top player in omnichannel payment solutions. Known for his expertise in devising successful marketing strategies that boost revenue and elevate brand presence, Josh's arrival is expected to drive Valor PayTech's growth and visibility in the industry.

news.headline: Integrity Warranty partners with PCMI, implements PCRS software to drive growth into used vehicle market.
news.summary: The utilization of PCMI's PCRS administration software has improved service capabilities and operations, allowing for greater expansion into the used vehicle market. This development occurred in PARK RIDGE, Illinois on October 17, 2024. PCMI (Policy Claim Management International), a prominent provider of administration services, has successfully integrated this software, resulting in enhanced performance. This implementation marks a significant advancement for the company as it seeks to further establish itself in the used vehicle market, indicating a strategic move toward growth and innovation.

news.headline: FlashGrid introduces new product for Oracle databases on AWS and Azure, enhancing reliability and uptime. Increases Data Guard FSFO foundation.
news.summary: FlashGrid has unveiled its newest product, the FlashGrid Server, designed to enhance the stability and availability of Oracle databases on cloud platforms like AWS and Azure. This cutting-edge solution aims to mitigate outages and establish a dependable infrastructure for Data Guard FSFO. The announcement was made in Sunnyvale, California, on October 17, 2024, through a press release on PRNewswire-PRWeb.

news.headline: Regan Hartley becomes VP of Sales at Energy CX to boost growth of the energy brokerage firm.

This move aims to bolster partnership growth within the firm. Hartley, with a background in energy technology and commercial real estate sales, brings valuable experience to the team. This strategic hire reflects Energy CX's commitment to expanding its market presence and driving sales.

news.headline: Prosidium Warranty & Capital appoints Mark Del Rosso as new CEO, Founder Travis Coulter to be Executive Chairman.
news.summary: Prosidium Warranty & Capital (PWC) has recently revealed a significant leadership change to drive its future expansion plans. Travis Coulter, the company's Founder and CEO for 15 years, will transition to the role of Executive Chairman. Stepping into the CEO position is Mark Del Rosso, who previously served as President of Audi America and CEO of Genesis Motors America. The transition marks a pivotal moment in the company's direction, aiming to capitalize on their expertise and drive growth under new leadership.

news.headline: Pasternack introduces new low-loss and low-PIM cable assemblies for enhanced signal quality and durability.
news.summary: Pasternack, a prominent provider of RF, microwave, and millimeter-wave products under Infinite Electronics brand, has unveiled new options for low-loss LMR and Low-PIM assemblies in Irvine, California on October 17, 2024. These new assemblies offer increased flexibility, durability, and signal integrity. This move is part of the company's strategy to consistently enhance its product offerings.

news.headline: Grant Cardone expands coaching program globally with new languages, aiding entrepreneurs and professionals. AVENTURA, Oct. 17, 2024.
news.summary: As of October 17, 2024, Grant Cardone is actively working on expanding multilingual support as a part of his continuous efforts to assist entrepreneurs, sales professionals, and organizations in achieving sustainable growth. The expansion is a key component of his Licensing Program, 10X Business Coach, based in Aventura, Florida. Grant Cardone's initiative aims to reach a broader audience and make a positive impact on a larger scale.

news.headline: HumanX partners with Section to host an AI Executive Leadership Workshop at inaugural conference in March 2025. #1 AI gathering. NY, PRNewswire, Oct. 17, 2024.
